reasoning: Microsporidiosis is an opportunistic infection caused by a group of spore-forming unicellular parasites (now classified as fungi) that primarily affects immunocompromised individuals (e.g., people with HIV/AIDS) or those in specific agricultural settings. Current treatment focuses on pharmacological therapies (like albendazole) and immune restoration (like antiretroviral therapy). There are absolutely no vaccines available or in late-stage clinical trials for Microsporidiosis.
